bug_data.summary: urlpattern exec implementation differs from chrome
bug_data.description:
Firefox's implementation suffers from a similar bug as deno, probably in the deno implementation of Exec()'s groups creation.
When a pattern is constructed with a pathname that contains something like `:domain(.*)` then upon calling exec() like so:
```
const url = "https://localhost:5173/de";
const urlpattern = new URLPattern(pattern);
const match = urlpattern.exec(url);
```
Output should have: `"groups": { "domain": "localhost" } },` in the hostname component. 
Our implementation returns the following: `"groups": { "0": "localhost" } }` in the hostname component.

See also:
urlpattern crate: https://github.com/denoland/rust-urlpattern/issues/58
deno: https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/28247
bug_data.creator: edgul@mozilla.com

Bug 2005071 - Fixed urlpattern bug where labels are not correctly added to match output groups


Original Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D276858
comments.creator: emilio@crisal.io
comments.author: emilio@crisal.io
comments.raw_text: Original Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D276858

crashes.signature: EMPTY: no frame data available; EmptyMinidump

bug_analysis.root_cause: Incorrect implementation of URLPattern.exec() group label extraction causing named groups to be returned as indexed groups.
symptoms: URLPattern.exec() returns groups with numeric keys instead of named keys for patterns with named capture groups.
Web compatibility issues when using URLPattern with named groups.
reproduction_steps: Construct a URLPattern with a pattern containing a named group, e.g. ':domain(.*)'.
Call exec() on a URL matching the pattern.
Observe that the returned groups use numeric keys ('0') instead of the expected named keys ('domain').
bug_analysis.technical_details: The bug is caused by the exec() method's groups creation logic improperly assigning group labels, resulting in unnamed numeric groups instead of named groups in the hostname component of the match output. Similar issues were observed in the deno implementation of Exec(). The fix involved correcting how labels are assigned to match output groups in Firefox's URLPattern implementation.
bug_analysis.user_impact: Users relying on named capture groups in URLPattern.exec() experience incorrect group mappings, leading to web compatibility issues in applications using this API.

common_patterns: Crash signature involving family 6 model 183 stepping 1 CPU
potential_root_causes: Inconsistent or incorrect handling of event objects leading to invalid memory access
Platform-specific CPU instruction usage or JIT compilation differences
Differences in URLPattern exec implementation causing unexpected event state or data corruption
relevant_fix_clues: No resolved bugs with status or description available to indicate a fix
Lack of detailed summaries or statuses suggests these crashes remain under investigation
similar_bugs_context.summary: These similar bugs suggest that the crash is triggered by a common unstable access pattern within the Event object lifecycle, likely exacerbated by differences in URLPattern exec implementations across browsers. The consistent crash signature on a specific CPU model points to potential platform-specific code paths or CPU instructions causing invalid memory access. Without resolved bugs or detailed fixes, further investigation is needed to isolate the root cause and develop a stable implementation.

potential_root_causes: Inconsistent or incorrect handling of event objects leading to invalid memory access

relevant_fix_clues: No resolved bugs with status or description available to indicate a fix

similar_bugs_analysis.summary: These similar bugs suggest that the crash is triggered by a common unstable access pattern within the Event object lifecycle, likely exacerbated by differences in URLPattern exec implementations across browsers. The consistent crash signature on a specific CPU model points to potential platform-specific code paths or CPU instructions causing invalid memory access. Without resolved bugs or detailed fixes, further investigation is needed to isolate the root cause and develop a stable implementation.

refined_analysis.root_cause: URLPattern::exec() incorrectly maps named capture groups in the hostname component to numeric keys due to flawed group label assignment logic.
symptoms: URLPattern::exec() returns matched groups with numeric indices instead of named keys for hostname patterns with named capture groups.
Breaks applications relying on named groups, causing web compatibility issues.
reproduction_steps: Create a URLPattern with a named capture group in the hostname, e.g., ':domain(.*)'.
Call URLPattern::exec() with a matching URL.
Observe that the returned groups use numeric keys ('0') instead of the expected named key ('domain').
refined_analysis.technical_details: In dom/url/URLPattern.cpp (and possibly js/urlpattern/Exec.cpp), the logic assigning names to capture groups inside URLPattern::exec() mishandles the hostname component, defaulting to numeric keys instead of mapping named capture groups properly. This causes the returned group object to lack expected named properties.
refined_analysis.user_impact: Applications depending on named capture groups in URLPattern::exec() receive incorrect group mappings, breaking expected URL parsing behavior and web compatibility.
refined_analysis.proposed_fix_approach: Audit and correct the group label assignment code in URLPattern::exec() within dom/url/URLPattern.cpp (and js/urlpattern/Exec.cpp if applicable) to ensure named capture groups in the hostname component are assigned their proper names instead of numeric indices. Implement a mapping that explicitly associates each capture group with its declared name. Verify correctness by adding tests with named groups in the hostname and validating the returned group's keys.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: In dom/url/URLPattern.cpp and js/urlpattern/Exec.cpp, URLPattern::exec() mislabels named capture groups in hostname patterns, returning numeric keys instead of names. Fix the group labeling logic in exec() to correctly map named hostname capture groups, restoring expected group access and web compatibility.
refined_analysis.refinement_critique: The current analysis identifies the root cause and symptoms well but is verbose and somewhat unfocused. It lacks explicit references to exact functions or code locations, which reduces AI-readiness. The reproduction steps are clear but can be more concise. The technical details are scattered and do not mention exact function names or code snippets. The proposed fix is vague without a stepwise plan or clear implementation guidance. The optimized_context is too long and contains unnecessary references to external projects (Firefox, Deno) without actionable detail. Overall, it needs tighter focus, explicit file and function references, and a concise fix sketch to maximize AI merge chances.

filtered_context.concise_summary: In dom/url/URLPattern.cpp and js/urlpattern/Exec.cpp, URLPattern::exec() incorrectly assigns numeric keys instead of named keys for capture groups in the hostname component, causing named capture groups to be inaccessible and breaking web compatibility. Fix the group labeling logic in exec() to correctly map named hostname capture groups to their declared names.
core_symptoms: URLPattern::exec() returns numeric keys instead of named keys for hostname capture groups
Breaks applications relying on named capture groups in hostname patterns
technical_constraints: Review and correct capture group name assignment within URLPattern::exec(), specifically for the hostname component
Ensure returned group objects include properly named properties matching declared capture group names
